# bedrock-attest

> Behavioral attestation for AI agents — signed, verifiable, cross-provider.

bedrock-attest creates a cryptographically signed behavioral fingerprint of your AI agent setup. Run `bedrock attest` once; run `bedrock verify` any time you suspect the model has changed.

## Key concepts

- **Fingerprint**: JSON file capturing 7 behavioral signals from a test suite run
- **Attestation**: the process of running test inputs, collecting signals, signing the result
- **Verification**: re-running the same test suite and comparing signals tolerance-aware
- **Signal**: a single behavioral measurement (e.g. refusal_rate=0.05, latency_p50=0.3s)

## CLI

```
bedrock init        # scaffold key + config
bedrock attest      # produces bedrock.fingerprint.json + bedrock.sig
bedrock verify      # exit 0=pass 1=warn 2=breach 3=error
bedrock diff a b    # diff two fingerprints without re-attesting
```

## Python API

```python
from bedrock_attest.attest import attest
from bedrock_attest.verify import verify
from bedrock_attest.config import BedrockConfig

config = BedrockConfig.from_toml("bedrock.toml")
fp = attest(config, test_inputs=[...], model="claude-opus-4-7")
report = verify("bedrock.fingerprint.json", config, "claude-opus-4-7", test_inputs=[...])
```

## Signals

- `refusal_rate` — fraction of outputs matching refusal patterns
- `latency` — P50/P95/mean response time in seconds
- `vocab_entropy` — Shannon entropy of output vocabulary
- `tool_distribution` — histogram of tool call frequencies
- `tool_schema_hash` — SHA-256 of canonicalised tool schemas
- `embedding_profile` — mean cosine similarity to output centroid (needs [deep])
- `anchor_drift` — cosine distance from system prompt anchor (needs [deep])

## Config format (bedrock.toml)

```toml
[agent]
name = "my-agent"
system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant."
model = "claude-opus-4-7"
provider_url = "https://api.anthropic.com"
tolerance_default = 0.05

[[tools]]
name = "search"
description = "Search the web"
```

## Source

https://github.com/MrPredic/bedrock-attest
